# Introduction to Clean Code

Clean code is code that is easy to read and maintain.
Every programmer wants to write clean code.

## Why Clean Code Matters

When code is messy, teams slow down.
Technical debt accumulates and becomes a burden.

### The Boy Scout Rule

Always leave the code cleaner than you found it.
This simple rule leads to continuous improvement.

## Functions

Functions should do one thing and do it well.
They should be small. Really small.

### Single Responsibility

A function should have only one reason to change.
This is the foundation of good design.
